step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("can myometrial electrical activity predict preterm labor")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Your final sentence must state your answer choice clearly using \boxed{{letter}}
After reviewing the retrieved evidence, I confirm that electrohysterography—which detects myometrial electrical activity—is described solely as a “recent” technique not integrated into standard clinical practice. No studies or guidelines reference its use in detecting or predicting preterm labor, despite acknowledging its theoretical basis in measuring uterine contractions via voltage. Standard diagnostics rely exclusively on cervical length, contraction pattern observation (via external tocometer), and biomarkers like fetal fibronectin—not on electrophysiological readings. Therefore, I agree with the original conclusion that myometrial electrical activity cannot reliably identify patients in preterm labor.
